files.content:
## When implementing auth-related code

Follow these rules for all authentication and authorization:

- Supported roles:
  - `SADM` â€” System Administrator
  - `PLCMT` â€” PLC Maintenance User
- Auth mechanism: JWT â€” credentials validated against Active Directory (LDAP); fallback to internal service-user DB, then MAB service on failure
- Protected endpoints use `@PreAuthorize("hasRole('SADM')")` or `@PreAuthorize("hasRole('PLCMT')")` Spring Security annotations

Never hardcode tokens, secrets, or credentials. Reference them by environment variable name only.

## When handling errors in API endpoints

All API responses use the `GenericResponse<T>` envelope â€” do not deviate:

```json
{
  "type": "ERROR",
  "code": "SOME_CODE",
  "description": "Human readable message"
}
```

Success responses follow the same envelope with `"type": "SUCCESS"` and a `data` field:

```json
{
  "type": "SUCCESS",
  "data": {}
}
```

Fields with `null` values are omitted from the JSON output (`@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)`).

When generating error handling code:
1. Set `type` to `"ERROR"` and provide a short descriptive `code`
2. Never expose stack traces or internal details in the `description`
3. Use `GenericResponse.createErrorResponse()` / `GenericResponse.createSuccessResponse(data)` factory methods

## When working with dates and times

- Format: ISO 8601 (`2024-03-18T10:22:00Z`)
- Timezone: Store and transmit as UTC everywhere
- Convert to local timezone only in UI display code, never in backend or API responses

## When using environment variables

- Naming: `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE`
- Secrets never appear in KB files â€” reference by name only (e.g. "uses `DATABASE_URL`")
- When adding a new env var, document it in this file's changelog

## Changelog

2026-04-11 â€” created
2026-04-11 â€” rewritten: removed 10 unfilled placeholders; filled roles (SADM/PLCMT), auth (LDAP JWT), and error format (GenericResponse) from source; removed unverified pagination and ID generation sections

## Summary

Shared Maven library (`linestop-serviceclient`) that provides a typed HTTP client for calling `ms-linestop-api-be`. Used by `admin-be` as a submodule dependency. Abstracts the REST calls to api-be behind a Java service interface so callers don't deal with raw HTTP.

Package root: `com.toyota.tme.linestopserviceclient.linestopapi`

---

## Classes

| Class | Role |
|---|---|
| `LinestopApiService` | Interface â€” defines the available api-be calls |
| `LinestopApiServiceImpl` | Implementation â€” uses `RestTemplate` to call api-be endpoints |
| `LinestopApiServiceConfig` | Spring `@Configuration` â€” configures the RestTemplate bean and base URL |
| `ApiClientRequestPayload` | Wrapper for outbound request body |
| `HttpRequestWrapper` | Low-level HTTP request helper |
| `RestTemplateDTO` | DTO carrying RestTemplate + metadata |

### Request DTOs

| DTO | Endpoint |
|---|---|
| `LinePerformanceRequestDTO` | Line KPI metrics request |
| `ProcessPerformanceRequestDTO` | Process KPI metrics request |
| `ProductionStatusRequestDTO` | Production run status request |

### Response / domain types

| Class | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `LineRunStatus` | Enum/value for line running state |

---

## Usage pattern

`admin-be` declares this library as a Maven dependency (via the `linestop-serviceclient` submodule). It injects `LinestopApiService` and calls it to retrieve performance data from `api-be` â€” e.g., for dashboard KPIs or background job calculations.

---

## Configuration

`LinestopApiServiceConfig` reads the api-be base URL from Spring properties (check `application.yml` in admin-be configuration). The dev proxy in admin-fe gives a hint that api-be runs on port 9004 in dev.

---

## Dependencies

- Spring Boot 3.4.7 (starter, data-rest â€” Jetty only, no Tomcat)
- Lombok 1.18.34
- Oracle JDBC ojdbc11 (for any direct DB calls if needed)
- paneumeasure-common 1.0.21

## Summary

Spring Boot 3.4.x backend (Jetty WAR) that exposes the operational Linestop REST API. Consumed by `admin-be` via `linestop-serviceclient` and potentially by other services. Provides real-time line performance, process performance, and production status data derived from the shared domain (Oracle DB via `linestop-domain`).

Package root: `com.toyota.tme.linestop.api`

---

## Endpoints (by controller)

| Area | Controller | Package |
|---|---|---|
| Authentication | `LoginController`, `TokenController`, `UserController` | `controller/authentication` |
| Health | `HealthController` | `controller/health` |
| Line performance | (feature) | `feature/lineperformance` |
| Process performance | (feature) | `feature/processperformance` |
| Production status | (feature) | `feature/productionstatus` |
| Line definitions | (feature) | `feature/line` |

---

## Feature areas

### Line performance (`feature/lineperformance`)
Returns KPI metrics per production line â€” OEE, downtime, takt. Consumed by `LinestopApiService` in `linestop-serviceclient` via `LinePerformanceRequestDTO`.

### Process performance (`feature/processperformance`)
Returns per-process performance metrics. Consumed via `ProcessPerformanceRequestDTO`.

### Production status (`feature/productionstatus`)
Returns current production run status (running / stopped / shift change). Consumed via `ProductionStatusRequestDTO`.

### Line (`feature/line`)
Line reference data service: `LineService` / `LineServiceImpl`.

---

## Authentication

Same JWT-based strategy as `admin-be`: LDAP / MAB / service token. Same `JwtSecurityFilter`, `SecurityConfig`, `JwtAuthenticationEntryPoint` classes replicated in `com.toyota.tme.linestop.api.common.config.authentication`.

---

## Module structure (Maven)

```
linestopapi-be-root (pom)
â”śâ”€â”€ paneumeasure-common   (submodule)
â”śâ”€â”€ linestop-domain       (submodule â€” shared JPA entities)
â”śâ”€â”€ cvqs-serviceclient    (submodule)
â”śâ”€â”€ web-app               (Spring Boot app)
â”śâ”€â”€ configuration         (Jetty/env config)
â””â”€â”€ maven-deployment      (Docker/k8s packaging)
```

---

## Consumers

- **admin-be** calls this service via `linestop-serviceclient` (`LinestopApiService`).
- Endpoint base path confirmed by serviceclient proxy config (port 9004 in dev).

## Purpose

Documents the technology stack across all five Linestop microservice modules. All backend services share a common Maven parent POM (`com.toyota.tme:master-pom:8.3`). The frontend is a standalone React/Vite SPA.

---

## Artifact Versions

Current release versions per module. Update this table on every version bump.

| Module | Artifact version |
|---|---|
| `ms-linestop-admin-be` | `2.0.18.23.linestop-admin-be-k8` |
| `ms-linestop-admin-fe` | `2.0.14.8.linestop-admin-fe-k8` |
| `ms-linestop-api-be` | `2.0.8.16.linestop-api-be-k8` |
| `ms-linestop-domain-be` | `1.0.2.LINESTOP-DOMAIN-BSUBR0` |
| `ms-linestop-serviceclient` | `1.0.4.LINESTOP-SERVICECLIENT-BSUBR0` |

---

## Rules

### Backend (Java â€” admin-be, api-be, domain-be, serviceclient)

| Concern | Technology | Version |
|---|---|---|
| Language | Java | 17 |
| Framework | Spring Boot | 3.5.12 (admin-be), 3.4.10 (api-be), 3.4.7 (domain-be, serviceclient) |
| Build | Maven (multi-module) | â€” |
| Parent POM | `com.toyota.tme:master-pom` | 8.3 |
| Servlet container | **Jetty** (Tomcat excluded) | 12.0.x |
| Persistence | Spring Data JPA + Oracle JDBC (`ojdbc11`) | 23.3.0.23.09 |
| Security | Spring Security + JWT (`jjwt`) | 0.11.5 |
| Scheduling | Spring Quartz | via spring-boot-starter-quartz |
| Caching | Ehcache 3 (jakarta classifier) | 3.10.8 |
| Logging | Log4j2 (spring-boot-starter-log4j2; Logback **excluded**) | â€” |
| HTTP client | Reactor Netty (reactor-netty-http) | 1.2.8 |
| Utilities | Lombok, Apache Commons Lang3 | 1.18.34, 3.18.0 |
| Internal lib | `paneumeasure-common` | 1.0.21.MEASURE-COMMON-BSUBR0 |
| Versioning scheme | `<major>.<minor>.<patch>.<module-name>-k8` or `-BSUBR0` | â€” |

### Frontend (admin-fe)

| Concern | Technology | Version |
|---|---|---|
| Language | JavaScript (JSX) â€” no TypeScript | â€” |
| Build/Dev | Vite + @vitejs/plugin-react-swc | 6.x |
| UI framework | Material UI v7 (@mui/material, @mui/x-data-grid, @mui/x-date-pickers) | 7.x |
| Routing | React Router DOM | v7 |
| Forms | React Hook Form + Yup | 7.x / 1.x |
| HTTP client | Axios | 1.13.5 |
| i18n | i18next + react-i18next | 24.x / 15.x |
| Dates | Day.js | 1.x |
| Excel export | ExcelJS + file-saver | 4.x |
| Notifications | react-toastify | 11.x |
| Styling | Emotion + styled-components | 11.x / 6.x |
| Virtualisation | @tanstack/react-virtual, react-virtuoso, react-window | mixed |
| Linting | ESLint 9 + Prettier | â€” |
| Pre-commit | Husky + lint-staged | 9.x |

---

## Why

- **Jetty over Tomcat**: explicit Tomcat exclusion in every backend module â€” the organisation deploys to Jetty containers (confirmed by `configuration/contexts/jetty-web.xml` in each service).
- **Log4j2 over Logback**: `spring-boot-starter-logging` excluded and `spring-boot-starter-log4j2` added â€” intentional choice for enterprise logging configuration.
- **Oracle DB**: `ojdbc11` present in domain-be and serviceclient â€” production data is on Oracle; no H2/Postgres alternative for runtime.
- **Spring Boot version drift**: admin-be is on 3.5.12 while api-be (3.4.10) and domain-be/serviceclient (3.4.7) lag behind. Not a deliberate split â€” upgrade is incremental per service.
- **JSX not TSX**: admin-fe uses `.jsx` files throughout with `@types/react` in devDependencies only â€” no TypeScript compilation is configured.

---

## Examples

**Tomcat exclusion (consistent across all backends)**
```xml
<!-- ms-linestop-domain-be/pom.xml -->
<exclusion>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-tomcat</artifactId>
</exclusion>
```

**Log4j2 adoption (consistent across all backends)**
```xml
<!-- ms-linestop-domain-be/pom.xml -->
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-log4j2</artifactId>
</dependency>
```

**Frontend dev proxy (admin-fe)**
```json
// ms-linestop-admin-fe/package.json
"proxy": "http://localhost:9004"
```

---

## Exceptions

- **Spring Boot versions are inconsistent** across services (3.4.7 / 3.4.10 / 3.5.12). This is an in-progress upgrade cadence, not intentional divergence. Standardise on 3.5.x.
- **Multiple virtualisation libraries** in admin-fe (`react-virtuoso`, `react-window`, `@tanstack/react-virtual`, `react-virtualized`). Likely accumulated over iterations; only one should be used going forward â€” prefer `@tanstack/react-virtual` or `react-virtuoso`.

## Summary

Spring Boot 3.5.x backend (Jetty WAR) for administration of the Linestop system. Exposes REST APIs consumed by `admin-fe`. Handles configuration of lines, buffers, users, parameters, PLC signals, process definitions, mail settings, and runs Quartz scheduled jobs. Authentication supports LDAP, MAB (external Toyota auth), and service-to-service JWT tokens.

Package root: `com.toyota.tme.linestop.admin`

---

## Domain areas

| Area | Controller | Feature package |
|---|---|---|
| Authentication | `LoginController`, `TokenController`, `UserController` | `common/service/auth` |
| Buffer definitions | `BufferDefinitionController` | `feature/buffer` |
| Line management | `LineController` | `feature/line` |
| Mail settings | `MailSettingsController` | `feature/mailsettings` |
| Manual records | `ManualRecordController` | `feature/manualrecord` |
| Parameters | `ParameterController` | `feature/parameter` |
| PLC signals | `PlcSignalsController` | `feature/plcsignals` |
| Process definitions | `ProcessDefinitionController` | `feature/processdefinition` |
| User definitions | `UserDefinitionController` | `feature/userdefinition` |
| Warning logs | `WarningLogsController` | `feature/warninglogs` |
| MAB options | (shared) | `feature/common/mab` |
| Quartz monitor | `QuartzMonitorController` | `feature/quartzmonitor` |
| Job status | (internal) | `feature/jobstatus` |

---

## Scheduled jobs (Quartz)

| Job | Class | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| CalcProductionStatus | `CalcProductionStatusJob` | Recalculates line/process production status |
| CheckPlcSignals | `CheckPlcSignalsJob` | Polls PLC signal states |
| CheckShift | `CheckShiftJob` | Checks and transitions shift changes |
| DeleteLogs | `DeleteLogsJob` | Purges old warning logs |

Jobs are configured via `*JobConfig` classes and monitored via `QuartzMonitorController`.

---

## Authentication strategy

Three authentication providers, selected at runtime:

1. **LDAP** (`LdapAuthenticationServiceImpl`) â€” Toyota corporate directory.
2. **MAB** (`MabAuthenticationServiceImpl`) â€” external Toyota manufacturing auth system.
3. **Service** (`ServiceAuthenticationServiceImpl`) â€” service-to-service calls using a JWT token.

JWT is issued on login (`JwtTokenUtil`) and validated per-request by `JwtSecurityFilter`. `JwtAuthenticationEntryPoint` returns 401 on invalid/missing tokens.

Security config: `SecurityConfig` (Spring Security).

---

## Key supporting infrastructure

- **Caching**: EhCache 3 via `CacheConfig` / `CacheEventLogger`.
- **Logging**: `AspectLogger` (AOP request/response logging), `IncidentLogger` (structured incident log).
- **Exception handling**: `GlobalExceptionHandler` (@ControllerAdvice), `MeasureSystemExceptionHandlerFilter`.
- **Datasource**: `RealDatasourceConfig` (Oracle), `AuditorAwareImpl` (JPA audit).
- **Counter service**: `CounterService` / `CounterServiceImpl` â€” auto-increment counter used for record ID generation.
- **MAB options**: `MabOptionsService` fetches reference data (defect classes, ranks, employees) from MAB system. Enum: `MabOptionType`.

---

## Module structure (Maven)

```
linestopadmin-be-root (pom)
â”śâ”€â”€ paneumeasure-common   (submodule â€” shared Toyota lib)
â”śâ”€â”€ linestop-domain       (submodule â€” JPA entities)
â”śâ”€â”€ cvqs-serviceclient    (submodule â€” CVQS HTTP client)
â”śâ”€â”€ linestop-serviceclient (submodule â€” api-be HTTP client)
â”śâ”€â”€ web-app               (Spring Boot app)
â”śâ”€â”€ configuration         (Jetty/env config, certificates)
â””â”€â”€ maven-deployment      (Docker/k8s packaging)
```

---

## Inter-service dependencies

- **linestop-serviceclient**: used to call `api-be` for performance/status data.
- **linestop-domain**: JPA entities shared with `api-be`.
- **cvqs-serviceclient**: calls the CVQS (Connected Vehicle Quality System) external service.
- **paneumeasure-common**: shared Toyota measurement platform utilities.

## Overview

This file documents all external system integrations for the Linestop application. Integrations are spread across `ms-linestop-admin-be`, `ms-cvqs-service-client`, and `ms-linestop-serviceclient`.

---

## 1. CVQS API

Service: `ms-cvqs-api-be` (Common Defect/Quality system)
Purpose: Defect management, PMS (Production Measurement System) updates, terminal definitions, part search
Auth: Token-based â€” POST `/token` with service credentials, token reused per session
Base URL: `external.cvqsapi.url`

| Environment | URL |
|---|---|
| Dev | `http://ms-cvqs-api-be.tmmt-dev-tmetk.svc.cluster.local:8080/cvqsapi/` |
| Acc | `http://ms-cvqs-api-be.tmmt-acc-tmetk.svc.cluster.local:8080/cvqsapi/` |
| Prod | `http://ms-cvqs-api-be.tmmt-prod-tmetk.svc.cluster.local:8080/cvqsapi/` |
| UK Test | `http://cvqsapiuka.toyotauk.com/cvqsapi/` |
| UK Prod | `http://cvqsapiuk.toyotauk.com/cvqsapi/` |

Credentials: env vars `measure.service.user.localId` / `measure.service.user.password`
Client: RestTemplate (via `ms-cvqs-service-client` module, component-scanned into admin-be)

---

## 2. Linestop API

Service: `ms-linestop-api-be`
Purpose: Production line status updates, line performance reporting
Auth: Token-based â€” POST `/api/authenticate` with service credentials
Base URL: `external.linestopapi.url`

| Environment | URL |
|---|---|
| Dev | `http://ms-linestop-api-be.tmmt-dev-tmetk.svc.cluster.local:8080/linestopapi-be/` |
| Acc | `http://ms-linestop-api-be.tmmt-acc-tmetk.svc.cluster.local:8080/linestopapi-be/` |
| Prod | `http://ms-linestop-api-be.tmmt-prod-tmetk.svc.cluster.local:8080/linestopapi-be/` |

Credentials: env vars `linestop.service.user.localId` / `linestop.service.user.password`
Client: RestTemplate (via `ms-linestop-serviceclient` module)

---

## 3. MAB Service (Mobile Application Backend)

Service: `ms-mab-service`
Purpose: User password changes â€” admin-be delegates password updates to MAB
Auth: none observed (plain HTTP call)
Base URL: `mab.rest.baseurl`

| Environment | URL |
|---|---|
| Dev | `http://ms-mab-service.tmmt-dev-tmetk.svc.cluster.local:8080/mabsrvc-web/rest` |
| Prod | `http://ms-mab-service.tmmt-prod-tmetk.svc.cluster.local:8080/mabsrvc-web/rest` |
| UK Test | `http://mabsrvctmuka.toyotauk.com/mabsrvc-web/rest` |

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| POST | `/user/changepassword/{employeeNo}/{oldPassword}/{newPassword}` | Delegate password change to MAB |

Client: RestTemplate (called directly from `UserController`)

---

## 4. Active Directory (LDAP)

Service: Corporate Active Directory
Purpose: Primary user authentication â€” validates credentials against AD before issuing a JWT
Auth: Simple LDAP bind (`username@domain`)
Protocol: JNDI LDAP (`com.sun.jndi.ldap.LdapCtxFactory`)

| Environment | URL | Domain |
|---|---|---|
| Dev | `ldap://adldap.secure.toyotatr.com:389` | `tmmt.manufacturing.eu.corp.toyota.com` |
| Prod | `ldaps://tradamfmcdc10.tmmt.manufacturing.eu.corp.toyota.com:3269` | `tmmt.manufacturing.eu.corp.toyota.com` |
| UK Test | `ldaps://UKBURMFMCDC10.tmuk.manufacturing.eu.corp.toyota.com:636` | `secure.toyotatr.com` |

Config properties: `ldapUrl`, `ldapDomain`, `ldapDomainEmc`

**AD error codes handled:**

| AD Code | Meaning | Handling |
|---|---|---|
| 525 | User not found | surface |
| 52e | Invalid credentials | surface |
| 530 | Not permitted to log in at this time | surface |
| 531 | Not permitted to log in from this workstation | surface |
| 532 | Password expired | surface |
| 533 | Account disabled | surface |

> [!caution] Auth fallback
> If LDAP auth fails, the system falls back to internal service-user DB, then MAB service. All three paths issue a JWT on success.

---

## 5. Oracle Database

Service: Oracle DB (`cvqsd` service name)
Purpose: Primary data persistence for all Linestop domain data
Driver: `oracle.jdbc.driver.OracleDriver` (`ojdbc11` 23.3.0.23.09)
JDBC URL (dev): `jdbc:oracle:thin:@(DESCRIPTION=(ADDRESS_LIST=(ADDRESS=(PROTOCOL=tcp)(HOST=lxtkoradbd04.tmmt.manufacturing.eu.corp.toyota.com)(PORT=1521)))(CONNECT_DATA=(SERVICE_NAME=cvqsd)))`
Username: `POKALINE`

Connection pool (C3P0):

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Initial / Min pool size | 2 |
| Max pool size | 10 |
| Acquire increment | 2 |
| Max statements | 300 |
| Pool name | `linestop-real` |

> [!caution] No alternative datasource
> There is no H2/Postgres fallback. Oracle is required for all environments including local dev.

---

## 6. SMTP (Email Notifications)

Service: Corporate SMTP relay
Purpose: Linestop event notifications sent by `SendLinestopMailJob`
Auth: None (open relay, port 25)

| Region | Host | Sender |
|---|---|---|
| TMMT Dev | `tksmtp.tmmt.com` | `linestop.dev_k8@toyotatr.com` |
| TMMT Acc | `tksmtp.tmmt.com` | `linestop.acc_k8@toyotatr.com` |
| TMMT Prod | `tksmtp.tmmt.com` | `linestop.prod_k8@toyotatr.com` |
| UK Test | `bxlnotes04.be.tmme.com` | `linestop.test@toyotatr.com` |

Config properties: `spring.mail.host`, `spring.mail.port`, `mail.enable`, `mail.recipient.limit` (100)

> [!caution] UK Test has mail disabled
> `mail.enable=false` on UK Test â€” no emails are sent in that environment.

---

## 7. Quartz Scheduler (JDBC-backed, clustered)

Service: Internal scheduler â€” not an external system, but uses the Oracle DB as its job store
Scheduler name: `ClusteredPaneuMeasureJobScheduler`
Job store: JDBC (`QRTZ_` table prefix), clustered (`isClustered=true`, check interval 6000ms)
Thread pool: SimpleThreadPool, 4 threads

| Job | Key | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `CalcProductionStatusJob` | CLPS | Calculates and updates production status |
| `CheckPlcSignalsJob` | CPLCS | Monitors PLC signal health |
| `CheckShiftJob` | â€” | Validates active shift information |
| `SendLinestopMailJob` | SM | Sends linestop event email notifications |
| `DeleteLogsJob` | â€” | Purges old log records |

---

## Open questions

- What is the token TTL / refresh strategy for CVQS API and Linestop API service tokens?
- Are CVQS API and Linestop API on the same auth server or separate?
- What is the MAB service auth mechanism (currently appears to be unauthenticated)?
- What are the Quartz job schedules (cron expressions) for each job?

## Changelog

2026-04-11 â€” created, derived from code exploration of ms-linestop-admin-be, ms-cvqs-service-client, ms-linestop-serviceclient
2026-04-11 â€” owner placeholder filled
